name: Tesla
research_goal: Evaluate Tesla as a potential supplier
summary: Tesla is a leading U.S.-based automotive and clean energy company with vertically integrated manufacturing, advanced battery technology, and a robust Supercharger network. Its strong financial performance, extensive R&D in electric drivetrains and autonomous systems, and scalable gigafactory capacity make it a compelling potential supplier for high‑performance battery cells, power electronics, software platforms, and related components.
products_or_services: Electric vehicle platforms (Model S, 3, X, Y, Cybertruck)
Battery packs (NCA, LFP) and 4680 battery cells
Autonomous driving hardware and software stack
Solar panels and solar roof tiles
opportunities: Secure supply of next‑generation 4680 battery cells for our EV roadmap
Leverage Tesla's battery management and thermal management software for our energy storage products
Co‑development of autonomous driving capabilities for our fleet
Access to Tesla's Supercharger network to enable faster charging for our vehicles
Potential joint venture or capacity sharing at Tesla gigafactories to increase our production volume
risks_or_missing_information: Production capacity constraints at Tesla gigafactories could limit reliable supply volumes
Tesla may prioritize its own vehicle orders, leading to higher pricing or limited availability for suppliers
Proprietary technology and limited willingness to share detailed IP may hinder integration
Geopolitical and regulatory risks (e.g., US‑China trade tensions, supply chain disruptions) affecting battery material availability
Insufficient publicly available data on Tesla's supplier qualification processes, contract terms, and financial health metrics
suggested_next_actions: Conduct a detailed supplier qualification audit of Tesla's battery cell and pack facilities, including capacity, cost structure, and lead times
Organize technical workshops to explore integration of Tesla's battery management and autonomous driving software into our products
Negotiate pilot agreements for 4680 cell supply and evaluate pricing and volume scenarios
Map alignment of Tesla's charging network with our vehicle rollout plans and assess feasibility of joint infrastructure investments
Gather additional financial, ESG, and risk data (e.g., credit rating, supply chain resilience) to refine the risk assessment
